Unfortunately, I was unable to verify the existence of the plant Adelia haemiolandra, however I can provide you with information about the plant Adelia ricinoides.

Adelia ricinoides, commonly known as Pus Carder, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various parts of the world, including the Caribbean and Central America, to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Adelia ricinoides include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Adelia ricinoides include ricinoides, a toxic compound with potential medicinal properties, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Adelia ricinoides include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
